title: Atote Labs
summary: Marketing and editorial website for Atote Labs, a Nairobi-based startup studio. Covers the studio's identity, its portfolio of ventures, insights and articles, and a contact system. Built with React 19, Vite, Tailwind CSS v4, and a Node.js/Express backend with PostgreSQL.
overview: Atote Labs is a startup studio based in Nairobi that builds software products from concept to launch. The website serves as the studio's public face — communicating its identity, introducing its portfolio of ventures, publishing insights and editorial content, and handling inbound inquiries. The platform has a React frontend backed by an Express API with PostgreSQL, with a light and dark theme toggle and a professional editorial design aesthetic.
problem: Startup studios need to project institutional credibility while remaining approachable to founders and collaborators. A generic template site would undermine the brand positioning. The website needed to feel like it was built by people who build things — opinionated in design, technically solid, and structured to grow as the studio's venture portfolio expands.
role: Designed and built the complete frontend in React 19 with React Router and Tailwind CSS v4
Built the Express backend with PostgreSQL and Prisma ORM for article and inquiry management
Implemented light and dark theme toggle with system preference detection
Structured the Ventures section to scale as new portfolio companies are added
Deployed the frontend to Cloudflare Pages and the backend to a managed Node.js environment
architecture: React 19 frontend with React Router for five-page navigation: Home, About, Ventures, Insights, Contact
Express.js API backend with PostgreSQL and Prisma ORM for content and inquiry persistence
ThemeContext for global light and dark mode with system preference detection
Tailwind CSS v4 with custom theme tokens for the studio's brand palette
VentureCard component system for scalable portfolio showcase
ArticleDetail page with structured content rendering for editorial insights

results.title: Studio Identity Platform
results.detail: Five-page site covering brand, ventures, editorial, and contact — giving Atote Labs a complete professional web presence.

results.title: Scalable Venture Showcase
results.detail: Component-driven portfolio section designed to grow as the studio's pipeline of products and companies expands.

results.title: Theme System
results.detail: Full light and dark mode with system preference detection and smooth transitions throughout.

lessonsLearned: Studio websites need room to breathe. Dense information or cluttered layouts signal a team that hasn't prioritized communication — the design has to be as considered as the work itself.
Editorial sections (articles, insights) need a clear content model from day one. Retrofitting a content schema after launch is significantly more expensive than planning it correctly upfront.
